item.text: I’ll execute exactly `turn-003` for P1. Assumption: since the required rules/config were read before P1’s first move, I’m using that same rules context and the current briefing as the source for this turn.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "printf '%s\\n' '{\"schemaVersion\":1,\"turnId\":\"turn-003\",\"player\":\"P1\",\"actions\":[{\"type\":\"playAction\",\"handIndex\":1},{\"type\":\"moveToBuy\"},{\"type\":\"buyCard\",\"cardId\":\"peddler\"},{\"type\":\"endTurn\"}]}' > ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/actions/turn-003.deck.json
bun run --silent cli -- deck-turn --config game/deck.yaml --state ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/deck.json --actions ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/actions/turn-003.deck.json --result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-003.deck.result.json
jq . ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-003.deck.result.json"

/bin/zsh -lc "printf '%s\\n' '{\"schemaVersion\":1,\"turnId\":\"turn-003\",\"player\":\"P1\",\"actions\":[{\"type\":\"playAction\",\"handIndex\":1},{\"type\":\"moveToBuy\"},{\"type\":\"buyCard\",\"cardId\":\"peddler\"},{\"type\":\"endTurn\"}]}' > ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/actions/turn-003.deck.json

item.text: Deck turn is complete: Blast produced 2 damage, and Peddler is the buy because it supports cycling/economy without worsening action pressure. I’m using the damage to remove P2’s forward scout while flipping more centers.

The board turn was accepted: P1 killed the P2 scout and flipped two additional centers. P1 still cannot recruit this turn because income takes supply from 2 to 5 before captures, below the 6 supply recruit cost.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "bun run --silent playtest -- commit-turn --run ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01 --deck-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-003.deck.result.json --board-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-003.board.result.json --summary \"P1 played Blast, bought Peddler, killed P2's forward scout, and expanded center control.\" --reasoning \"Played Blast for 2 deck damage and bought Peddler at 4 to add cycling and economy without creating terminal-action clog. On the board, P1-raider-start-1 moved to 6,4 to attack the P2 scout on center-center, P1-scout-start-2 moved to 7,5 to capture center-center-south, P1-raider-start-3 moved to 5,3 to capture center-center-north, and P1-marksman-start-4 moved to 7,3 for ranged follow-up. Raider and marksman each used 1 deck damage into P2-scout-start-2, removing it and weakening P2's center control. No recruit was legal because P1 had only 5 supply after income.\" --strict-win
bun run --silent validate-run -- --strict --strict-deck --strict-win ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/timeline.json"

/bin/zsh -lc "bun run --silent playtest -- commit-turn --run ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01 --deck-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-003.deck.result.json --board-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-003.board.result.json --summary \"P1 played Blast, bought Peddler, killed P2's forward scout, and expanded center control.\" --reasoning \"Played Blast for 2 deck damage and bought Peddler at 4 to add cycling and economy without creating terminal-action clog. On the board, P1-raider-start-1 moved to 6,4 to attack the P2 scout on center-center, P1-scout-start-2 moved to 7,5 to capture center-center-south, P1-raider-start-3 moved to 5,3 to capture center-center-north, and P1-marksman-start-4 moved to 7,3 for ranged follow-up. Raider and marksman each used 1 deck damage into P2-scout-start-2, removing it and weakening P2's center control. No recruit was legal because P1 had only 5 supply after income.\" --strict-win
bun run --silent validate-run -- --strict --strict-deck --strict-win ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/timeline.json"
